examples: A single e-scooter visible just below the surface.
A single plastic bottle drifting along the embankment.
Shopping cart half-submerged near the bridge piling.
Unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow.
Surface entirely covered in green-blue scum — full bloom.
Dense surface mat covering roughly a third of the visible water.
Patches of greenish water near the houseboat moorings.
Iridescent rainbow sheen across a moderate area near a moored boat.

narrative.intro: Nine approved reports this week, up from zero the previous period. The main pattern is a mix of surface debris and significant algae blooms, with the Prinsengracht seeing the most activity.

by_type.trash: Reports from the Prinsengracht, Grimburgwal, and Krom Boomssloot described submerged e-scooters, a drifting plastic bottle and a half-submerged shopping cart; these are mostly minor issues.
by_type.other: One report from the Prinsengracht noted unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow, which is a concerning observation.
by_type.blooming: Significant blooms were spotted on the Prinsengracht, Snoekjesgracht, and Rokin, ranging from greenish patches to a full surface cover of blue-green scum.
by_type.petroleum: A moderate iridescent rainbow sheen was reported near a moored boat on the Prinsengracht, which appears to be a minor incident.

narrative.outlook: Temperatures remain cool, with highs staying below 16 °C and rainfall between 0.0 and 2.1 mm daily. These conditions should slow or pause the expansion of the reported algae blooms.
narrative.thanks: Thank you to the nine citizens who sent in their reports this week. Your observations help us keep a clear picture of the water's health. To everyone reading: when you spot something off in the canals, please send the next report. 💧
